p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, consistent leaf count.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Remained stable throughout the observation period.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Healthy; no signs of chlorosis or wilting.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot, central position, two primary leaves.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Gradual decline in turgidity observed over 5 days.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Stressed; leaf margins show signs of dehydration.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, white rabbit anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: Leaf orientation shifted slightly; apical leaf shows minor necrosis.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Moderate stress; necrosis at leaf tip suggests potential over-watering or nutrient imbalance.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Black pot, small sprout near rim.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Growth has been stagnant; minimal development observed.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Dormant/Stunted; lack of new leaf production.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil in p3 and p4 shows increased white mineral/salt crusting over time.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: White granular debris (likely perlite or salt buildup) increased significantly in p3/p4 by T-1.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Initial stability followed by onset of soil surface crusting.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_Current: Increased accumulation of white particulate matter on soil surface; p3 leaf necrosis progression.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: Overall biome health is declining due to potential soil compaction and salt accumulation. Pothos (p3) is the primary indicator of environmental stress.
vision_report.anomalies: Significant white particulate accumulation in p3/p4 pots; possible mineral salt buildup from water evaporation.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment shows signs of environmental stress. While the String of Nickels (p1) remains robust, the plants in the black pots (p2, p3, p4) are struggling. The most notable change is the appearance and accumulation of white mineral deposits on the soil surface, which correlates with the observed leaf necrosis in the Pothos (p3). The Mexican Mint (p2) is showing signs of reduced turgor pressure, likely due to the same soil-level stressors.
